description: A segment of customers with high page views, consistent add-to-cart activity, and frequent purchases across diverse categories. All customers in this segment have made purchases, with a strong alignment between viewed and purchased categories. They exhibit moderate spending and have not engaged recently, with long gaps since their last session and order.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 30.2%; Beauty: 25.1%; Toys: 15.2%
Top purchase categories: Books: 30.2%; Beauty: 25.1%; Toys: 15.2%
cautions: Segment produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores; treat as a behavior-based approximation, not a sharply separated natural group
Avoid overconfident or marketing-style labels due to clustering uncertainty

description: A segment of customers who frequently browse and add items to cart but never complete purchases, with activity concentrated in specific categories and no recorded orders.
evidence: Avg days since last order: none
Avg dominant purchase category ratio: none
Top view categories: Books: 20.2%; Beauty: 19.0%; Toys: 13.8%
cautions: Segment derived from KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating approximate behavior-based grouping
No purchase-related metrics available for analysis
Dominant purchase category ratio and purchase category diversity cannot be determined

description: A segment of customers who exhibit high engagement through frequent page views and add-to-cart actions, but make purchases infrequently and with long intervals between sessions and orders. They have a high purchaser ratio but low purchase frequency, with spending concentrated in a few dominant categories that partially align with their browsing behavior.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 23.4%; Beauty: 18.5%; Toys: 17.0%
Top purchase categories: Books: 26.8%; Beauty: 23.5%; Toys: 15.2%
cautions: This segment was produced by KMeans clustering with generally low silhouette scores, so it should be treated as a behavior-based approximation rather than a sharply separated natural group.
Avoid overconfident or marketing-style labels due to the approximate nature of the clustering.

description: A segment of customers who view many pages, add items to cart at a moderate rate, and consistently purchase across sessions. They exhibit high purchase category focus and perfect view-purchase category alignment, with spending concentrated in Books, Beauty, and Toys.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 26.2%; Beauty: 21.7%; Toys: 15.6%
Top purchase categories: Books: 26.2%; Beauty: 21.7%; Toys: 15.6%
cautions: Segment derived from KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores - represents behavior-based approximation, not distinct natural group
No evidence about income, age, occupation, family status, or lifestyle
Avg days since last order (983.8) suggests potential inactivity bias in timing metrics

description: A large segment of customers who frequently browse and add items to cart, consistently purchase across diverse categories, but show no overlap between their most-viewed and most-purchased categories. They exhibit moderate purchase frequency and spend, with extended inactivity periods between sessions and orders.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 18.6%; Toys: 17.2%; Beauty: 15.8%
Top purchase categories: Beauty: 24.2%; Books: 23.5%; Toys: 14.1%
cautions: Segment derived from KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores - represents approximate behavioral grouping
No demographic or psychographic inferences can be made from available data
Category match rate of 0.0% indicates statistical separation but not necessarily intentional behavioral distinction

segment_name: Low-Frequency Purchasers with Narrow Purchase Focus
description: A segment of customers who purchase infrequently, with high concentration in a dominant purchase category, despite moderate browsing activity across multiple categories. They have not made a purchase in over two years but consistently add items to cart during sessions.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 19.2%; Beauty: 15.9%; Toys: 15.0%
Top purchase categories: Books: 21.8%; Beauty: 20.1%; Toys: 15.9%
cautions: Segment produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating approximate behavior-based grouping rather than distinct natural clusters
No direct correlation between viewed and purchased categories (0.0% match rate)
Long inactivity period since last order (1043.7 days) suggests dormant purchasing behavior
